lesson: When several codebase versions share one core library, put the divergent version's vendored headers earlier on its include path so they shadow the shared headers, scoped to that translation unit only.

When unifying divergent codebase variants behind a shared engine, pick one variant as the byte-identical reference and gate every shared-code commit with an automated golden/identity test, not a policy note.

In cross-version wire serializers, distinguish fields present only in the canonical model (omit from wire) from fields present only on the wire (need default synthesis) — conflating the two corrupts the payload.
Before assuming a shortcut refactor exists across N codebase variants, run an empirical pairwise duplication study first; the real overlap can turn out to be zero identical files.
condition_variable wait_until/wait_for keyed on steady_clock can hang forever if the OS steps the wall clock backward; use relative-timeout waits for anything that must not freeze.
Two network services that both default to the same port can silently co-bind via SO_REUSEADDR; kill stale listeners before starting a new one or clients connect to the wrong process.

When cloning production databases into a parallel unified environment, only bump version/schema-check rows to satisfy the new code's checks; never alter schema, so the originals stay untouched rollback points.
